Nixie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
Any Champions you use with the DPS role must have a CHA of 17+.

items.name: If the Trees are Thin, Let the Flames Begin!
restrictions: Must have completed "The Grandfather Acorn (Nixie)" Costs 625 Leaves to start

Nixie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
Nixie's Wild Magic Surge ability starts out unlocked.
Nixie attacks faster as her base attack cooldown is reduced by 2 seconds.
Nixie's base attacks sometimes accidentally light a random Champion on fire, damaging them for 20% of their max health.
Getting to Know Nixie: Nixie's attacks often come with unexpected results. Check out her Wild Magic Surge effects!

items.name: The Limitless Possibilities of Nixie
restrictions: Must have completed "The Grandfather Acorn (Nixie)" Costs 1250 Leaves to start

Nixie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions that are Tieflings, Chaotic, or have a total ability score of 78 or lower.
Getting to Know Nixie: Nixie's specialization bonuses depend on the other Champions in the formation. Pick a specialization and build your formation around it to maximize your damage!

items.name: Free Play (Nixie)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Grandfather Acorn (Nixie)" Costs up to 2500 Leaves to start
No restrictions

Only Neutral (Good/Evil axis) Champions can deal damage.

Evandra starts in the formation. She can't be moved or removed.
If you have them and they're not already on another adventure, Antrius and Nixie start unlocked and in the formation.
1-2 Mechanical Guards spawn with every wave. They don't drop gold, nor do they count towards quest progress.
Construct enemies move at 300% speed and deal 300% damage.
Getting to Know Evandra: Evandra is the Tank of the Awful Ones affiliation. Use them all to repel these mechanical menaces!

items.name: That's What Friends Are For
restrictions: Must have completed "The Mechanical Menace (Evandra)" Costs 1250 Rusted Gears to start

Evandra starts in the formation. She can't be moved or removed.
You may only use Champions that are female, nonbinary, fighters, rangers, barbarians, paladins, or have a total ability score of 78 or less.
Getting to Know Evandra: Evandra's specialization bonuses depend on the other Champions in the formation. Pick the best specialization for your formation!

items.name: Free Play (Evandra)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Mechanical Menace (Evandra)" Costs up to 2500 Rusted Gears to start
No restrictions

restrictions: Must have completed "Let Sleeping Dragons Lie (BBEG)" Costs 250 Dragon Claws to start

BBEG starts in the formation and can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use BBEG and/or Champions with an Intelligence of 12 or lower
Getting to Know BBEG: BBEG increases the damage of his less intelligent minions, like zombies.

BBEG starts in the formation and can be moved, but not removed.
Every second, a cat spawns and moves towards a random champion. When the cat reaches the champion, they are stunned for 3 seconds because of overwhelming cuteness. The cats can't be attacked.
Stunned Champions can not attack, but their formation abilities remain enabled.
Getting To Know Pat: Pat is the long-suffering Dungeon Master for the Awful Ones and runs all the NPCs, including the BBEG. Herd those cats and stop this homebrew adventure from going off the rails!

items.name: It Was Me All Along!
restrictions: Must have completed "Let Sleeping Dragons Lie (BBEG)" Costs 1250 Dragon Claws to start

BBEG starts in the formation and can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Lawful Champions, Evil Champions, or Champions with a total ability score of 78 or less.
Getting to know BBEG: BBEG's specialization bonuses depend on the other Champions in the formation. Pick a specialization and build your formation around it to maximize your damage!

items.name: Free Play (BBEG)
restrictions: Must have completed "Let Sleeping Dragons Lie (BBEG)" Costs up to 2500 Dragon Claws to start
No restrictions

items.name: Oath of Justice

Strongheart starts in the formation and can be moved, but not removed.
Only Strongheart and Champions next to him can deal damage.

No restrictions

items.name: Fangs for the Memories

1-100: Lawful or Good. 101-200: Neutral or Good. 201-300: Chaotic or Good.  301-400: Chaotic or Neutral. 401-500: Chaotic or Evil. 501-600: Neutral or Evil. 601-700: Lawful or Evil. 701-800: Lawful or Neutral. Then back to 801-900: Lawful or Good.
This restriction is linked to your highest area completed, not your current area.

restrictions: Must have completed

You may only use Champions that are a Standard species. Standard species are Aasimar, Dragonborn, Dwarf, Elf, Gnome, Goliath, Halfling, Human, Orc, Tiefling, Half-Orc, and Half-Elf.
A Drow Priestess joins the formation. 
Each wave, 1-2 Giant Spiders attack. When defeated, they respawn in the same location after 5 seconds unless defeated by a Champion next to the Drow Priestess.

items.name: The Fiery War of Pandesmos
restrictions: Must have completed

You may only use Champions that are NOT a Standard Species. Standard species are Aasimar, Dragonborn, Dwarf, Elf, Gnome, Goliath, Halfling, Human, Orc, Tiefling, Half-Orc, and Half-Elf.
1-2 fiery foes spawn with each wave. These enemies have 300% speed, and they don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed
No restrictions

items.name: The Ruinous Bosses
restrictions: Must have completed

You may only use Champions that are not a Standard species. Standard species are Aasimar, Dragonborn, Dwarf, Elf, Gnome, Goliath, Halfling, Human, Orc, Tiefling, Half-Orc, and Half-Elf.
Aarakocra Champions break 3 times the amount of armored or segmented health when they deal damage.
Bosses cannot be moved, rooted, or stunned, except in extraordinary circumstances, and they immediately start to enrage upon spawning.
All boss monsters have at least 25 armored health.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed
No restrictions

items.name: Betraying the Betrayer
restrictions: Must have completed "Vecna's Grasp"

You may only use Champions with a total ability score of 80 or higher.
Every 50 areas, the Champion with the lowest base DPS betrays the party and leaves the formation for the rest of the adventure. This does not affect Kas if he is in the formation. If Kas has the lowest DPS, the Champion with the second lowest DPS leaves instead.
After area 25, you can't otherwise remove Champions from the formation.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed
No restrictions

items.name: Lending a Hand and Eye
restrictions: Must have completed "Shattered Reflection"

Undead enemies speed and damage are increased by 300%.
The Eye and Hand of Vecna join the formation. You may only place Wizard Champions next to the Eye, and you may only place Champions with a Strength of 18 or higher next to the Hand.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed
No restrictions

items.name: Hunting For A Good Meal
restrictions: Must have completed "An Acquired Taste"

Ezmerelda starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. Only Champions with the Hunter role (like Ezmerelda or Minsc) can deal damage. You can still use other Champions to tank, support, or heal, they just won't deal damage. 1-2 Zombies loyal to Strahd spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Enemies have 1000% more health, but enemies that are a Favored Foe for any Champion take 400% additional damage. Getting to know Idle Champions: Champions with the Hunter role usually have Favored Foes, granting your Champions an advantage against enemies of specific types.

items.name: Swimming In Debuff
restrictions: Must have completed "An Acquired Taste"

Ezmerelda starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with the Debuff role.
Enemies without a debuff applied to them only take 1 damage from all attacks, move 400% faster, and deal 800% more damage.

items.name: Breaker Breaker
restrictions: Must have completed "An Acquired Taste"

Ezmerelda starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
Champions without the Breaker role have their base damage permanently set to 1.
An ogre carrying a battering ram takes up 3 slots in the formation.
All boss enemies have 25 armored health if they don't have segmented or armored health already.
Strahd spawns as an extra boss in every boss area. He must also be defeated to progress.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed "An Acquired Taste"
No restrictions

items.name: The Pugilists That Push
restrictions: Must have completed "The Ties That Bind"

Catti-brie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. Enemies take significantly less damage the closer they get to the formation. Use Champions that knock back enemies to get around this problem! Enemies have 1000% more health, but this buff is decreased by 100% for each Control Champion you have in your formation, stacking additively. Getting to know Idle Champions: Some Champions with the Control role can push enemies away from the formation, protecting your front line.

items.name: The Champions That Crit
restrictions: Must have completed "The Ties That Bind"

Catti-brie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
Champions have an additional 20% critical hit chance.
Champions' normal attacks only deal damage when they are Critical Hits.
Two local residents join the formation to experience life as  adventurers. They're a little battle-shy and don't attack or assist in any way.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 50.

items.name: The Fodder That Fights
restrictions: Must have completed "The Ties That Bind"

Catti-brie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use unaffiliated Champions and/or Companions of the Hall Champions.
Each unaffiliated Champion in the formation adds 1 Mithral Hall stack.
Every 75 areas, up to two random Champions are removed from the formation. They can't be used for the rest of the adventure. Champions from the Companions of the Hall affiliation are unaffected by this.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 75.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed "The Ties That Bind"
No restrictions

items.name: An Arranged Welcome
restrictions: Must have completed "A Royal Welcome"

Melf starts in the formation. He can't be moved or removed. You may only use Champions that have positional formation abilities. You may not place Champions with the DPS role next to Melf. Getting to know Idle Champions: A positional formation ability is an ability that applies to only some Champions in specific slots in a formation.

items.name: A Mixed Welcome
restrictions: Must have completed "A Royal Welcome"

Melf starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Only one Champion of each species can be in the formation at any time. Multispecies Champions count for all of their species. You may use a second elf in addition to Melf.
Quest requirements are doubled on all non-boss areas.

items.name: An Acidic Welcome
restrictions: Must have completed "A Royal Welcome"

Melf starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use one Champion with the DPS role.
If you add a male DPS Champion to the formation, you may not use any other male Champions (other than Melf.)
If you add a female DPS to the formation, you may not use any other female Champions.
A nonbinary DPS allows anyone to be used, and a nonbinary support Champion may always be used.
Acid Bubbles spawn in all non-boss areas. Some bubbles contain additional enemies that must be defeated afterwards. When a bubble is popped (killed), all Champions in the formation take 5% of their maximum health as acid damage. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed "A Royal Welcome"
No restrictions

items.name: For Vengeance
restrictions: Must have completed "Two Skies"

Lae'zel starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. After area 50, Champions only deal 1 point of damage with normal attacks that aren't Critical Hits. A lesser Gibbering Mouther spawns with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Some Champions, like Lae'zel, Strongheart, and Catti-brie, have an increased chance to deal high-damage Critical Hits with their attacks.

items.name: For Resolve
restrictions: Must have completed "Two Skies"

Lae'zel starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Wisdom of 14 or higher, and/or Dexterity of 16 or higher. You may also use Commodore Krux.
A Githyanki and Githzerai join the formation. Champions between them deal no damage due to their bickering.
An Intellect Devourer spawns with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

items.name: For Victory
restrictions: Must have completed "Two Skies"

Lae'zel starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Evil and/or Neutral Champions. You may also use Commodore Krux. 
After area 50, Champions only deal 1 point of damage with normal attacks that aren't Critical Hits.
Enemy speed and damage is increased by 300%.
An armored Mind Flayer Guard spawns with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed "Two Skies"
No restrictions

items.name: A Good Tank Is Hard To Find
restrictions: Must have completed "Returning Day"

Dynaheir starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. Other than Krydle, you may not use Tanking Champions. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Tanking Champions keep your formation safe when things get dangerous. Use Dynaheir's specialization choice wisely if you need help finding tanks!

items.name: Threading the Needle
restrictions: Must have completed "Returning Day"

Dynaheir starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Constitution of exactly 14, not counting any Circle Magic shenanigans.

items.name: Nom Nom Nom
restrictions: Must have completed "Returning Day"

Dynaheir starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. The Devourer joins the formation. After area 50, the Devourer eats the first quest item dropped by each defeated enemy. Getting to Know Idle Champions: The Devourer is consuming the first Quest Item that each enemy drops. If only there were ways to increase that number?

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed "Returning Day"
No restrictions

items.name: An Event Worth Remembering
restrictions: Must have completed "Friends of a Feather"

Strongheart starts in the formation with his Justice Needs Champions ability unlocked. He can't be moved or removed. You may only use Event Champions. You can get more Event Champions in events, from time gates, or in this campaign! Only Strongheart and Champions affected by his Justice Needs Champions ability can deal damage. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Events are the easiest way to collect and power up your Champions, and Strongheart's scavenger ability gets you additional Event tokens for use in these events!

items.name: The Critical Hits Strike Back
restrictions: Must have completed "Friends of a Feather"

Strongheart starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Good Champions.
After area 50, Champions deal 1 damage with normal attacks until any Champion lands a Critical Hit in the current area.
When a Champion deals a Critical Hit, that Champion takes damage equal to 20% of their maximum health.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 50.

items.name: An Affiliation Affair
restrictions: Must have completed "Friends of a Feather"

Strongheart starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Only Strongheart and Champions from the affiliation(s) with the most members in the formation can deal damage.
Once the formation has 3 Champions from the same affiliation, you can't add any more Champions from that affiliation.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: Must have completed "Friends of a Feather"
No restrictions

items.name: Double Standards
restrictions: Havilar joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions that are a Standard Species. Standard Species are Aasimar, Dragonborn, Dwarf, Elf, Gnome, Goliath, Halfling, Human, Orc, Tiefling, Half-Orc, and Half-Elf Quest requirements are doubled. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Some Speed Champions, like Havilar, increase the rate at which you progress in area quests. Use her and other Speed Champions to get through these increased requirements.

items.name: Chipping Away
restrictions:
Havilar joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with an ability score total of 81 or more.
All enemies have 4 armored health if they don't already have segmented health.
Enemy speed and damage are increased by 100%.
All Champions' base attack cooldowns are increased by 3 seconds.

items.name: Charging Up
restrictions:
Havilar joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Neutral Good, Chaotic Good, and/or Chaotic Neutral Champs.
Each enemy's damage increases by 100% for each second they are active before they attack, stacking multiplicatively.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: How Low Can You Go?
restrictions: Hank joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions with an ability score total of 78 or less. Once you have two Champions in the formation with a particular ability score total, you may not add any more Champions with that particular ability score total. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Many variants restrict Champions based on their ability scores. By collecting a bunch of Champions, you'll have more choices in these restrictive variants.

items.name: One Hit Wonders
restrictions:
Hank joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions that are 30 years old or younger. Additionally, you may use Rosie and/or Gromma.
All enemies in a wave spawn at the same time.
Enemies with melee attacks automatically defeat Champions they deal damage to.

items.name: Crossover Episode
restrictions:
Hank joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Good Champions.
Assorted friends join the formation.
At the start of each Boss area, Venger arrives on his nightmare as an additional boss that must be defeated.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: Uneclipsed
restrictions: Kas joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may not use any other Champions that have the DPS role. One of Kas's soldiers joins the formation. Only Evil Champions next to the soldier can deal damage with their base attacks. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Champions with the DPS role are your main damage dealers. Choose and position your Champions well to make the most out of your party's main damage dealer!

items.name: Who's the Boss?
restrictions:
Kas joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Boss health, damage, and speed are increased by 100%.
A boss enemy spawns at the start of the first wave of each non-boss area. These extra bosses do not drop gold or loot bags, or count toward quest progress
Two of Kas's soldiers join the formation. Only Evil Champions next to either soldier can deal damage with their base attacks.

items.name: No Rest for the Wicked
restrictions:
Kas joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. 
Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health.
At the start of each area, Champions take damage equal to 30% of their max health.
Three of Kas's soldiers join the formation. Only Evil Champions next to either soldier can deal damage with their base attacks.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: Gone Missing
restrictions: Nordom joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Click damage only deals damage for the first 25 areas. Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure. Once a Champion is placed in the formation they can not be swapped or removed Every 50 areas, 20% of the Champions are removed and locked for the remainder of the adventure. Nordom is immune to this effect. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Once you've unlocked at least two Champions in each bench slot, you can attempt to unlock a second party and run two sets of Champions at the same time! This variant gives you a little practice on how to Split the Party.

items.name: Clockwork Clash
restrictions:
Nordom joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
Only one Champion of each species can be included in the formation at any time. Champions with multiple species count for all of their species.
Non-boss Construct enemies without segmented health have 4 segmented health instead of their normal health
1-2 Modrons spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.

items.name: All Roads Lead to R04M
restrictions:
Nordom joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a total ability score of 82 or higher.
R04M starts in the formation. Champions next to R04M have a +2 bonus to all ability scores, but it's too late to help with the variant restriction.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: Rise of a Legacy
restrictions: Duke Ravengard starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions with a Melee base attack. After you complete area 50 for the first time, you can't remove Champions from the formation. When you complete this variant, any Champions in the formation will be unavailable in the Burden of Legacy or End of a Legacy variants. Each Patron variant has its own list of unavailable Champions. Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 100. Getting to Know Idle Champions: A Champion's attack is categorized as a Melee, Ranged, and/or a Magic attack. Some Variants and Champion abilities require a Champion to have a specific type of attack!

items.name: Burden of Legacy
restrictions: Duke Ravengard starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions with a Melee base attack. You also can't use any Champions that you used to finish variant 1 other than Duke Ravengard. After area 10, all enemies spawn with an extra 10 armored hit points which must be destroyed before their regular hit points appear. When an enemy is knocked back, these armored hit points are immediately removed, revealing its normal health bar. After you complete area 50 for the first time, you can't remove Champions from the formation. When you complete this variant, any Champions in the formation will be unavailable in the End of a Legacy variant. Each Patron variant has its own list of unavailable Champions. Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 100.

items.name: End of a Legacy
restrictions:
Duke Ravengard starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Melee base attack. You also can't use any Champions that you used to finish variant 1 and 2 other than Duke Ravengard.
After area 10, enemies spawn with a special shield which must be destroyed before their regular hit points appear. To destroy a special shield, an enemy must be hit with a critical hit.
Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure, and click damage has no effect after area 100.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: Kobold Without a Cause
restrictions: Spurt starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Only Champions that aren't part of an affiliation can be used. Getting to Know Idle Champions: An Affiliation is a small group of Champions that often have mechanics that encourage using them together. Unaffiliated Champions often have flexible abilities that can be used in many different formations.

items.name: The Fast and the Flattened
restrictions:
Spurt starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions that have a base attack cooldown of 4 seconds or less.
All Enemies move 100% faster and their base attack cooldowns are reduced by 50%.

items.name: Wasps and the Kobold that Loves Them
restrictions:
Spurt starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with both a Dexterity and Charisma of 13 or higher.
Two Hellwasps join the formation. They just get in the way.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: Power Word Pain
restrictions: Baeloth joins the formation. He can't be moved or removed. Only Baeloth and the Champions in the columns ahead and behind him can deal damage with their base attacks. Each time a Champion other than Baeloth deals damage with their base attack, they take damage equal to 5% of their max health. Each time a Champion other than Baeloth uses their ultimate, they take damage equal to 50% of their max health. Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Some Champions like Baeloth can prevent a Champion from dying. This often provides your formation with the final push you need to succeed!

items.name: Power Word Stun
restrictions:
Baeloth joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with the Gold Find, Hunter, or DPS role.
After area 50, one Champion is stunned in each area. The Champion in seat 1 is stunned first, advancing by bench order in each area. Baeloth's seat is skipped, and this resets after seat 12.
Champions in stunned seats cannot be moved or removed from the formation.

items.name: Power Word Kill
restrictions:
Baeloth joins the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
After area 50, a random Champion other than Baeloth takes damage equal to 100% of their max health every 10 seconds.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: Three Wolf Moon
restrictions: Shadowheart joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. In non-boss areas, 3 Wolves spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. These Wolves are armored enemies that move 200% faster and deal 400% more damage. Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health. Getting to Know Idle Champions: Champions with the Healing role let other heroes recover health or gain temporary health that can go above normal maximums.

items.name: Waxing or Waning
restrictions:
Shadowheart joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions with a Dexterity of 14 or higher.
If the formation includes an Evil Champion, you may not add Good Champions.
If the formation includes a Good Champion, you may not add Evil Champions.
Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area.
Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health.

items.name: Can't Cast a Shadow Without Some Light
restrictions:
Shadowheart joins the formation. She can be moved, but not removed.
Other than Shadowheart, you may only use Champions with a Dexterity of 13 or lower.
A Torch joins the formation and bathes all adjacent formation slots in bright Light. Formation slots not adjacent to the torch are cast into Shadow.
Champions in the Light take 5% of their max health as damage every second and can not deal damage with their base attacks.
Champions in the Shadow have their damage boosted by 1000% for each Champion that is in the Light, stacking multiplicatively.
Whenever Shadowheart changes her position in the formation, the torch occupies her previous position (and if she took the spot of another Champion, they are moved to where the torch was).
Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. 
Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health.

items.name: Free Play
restrictions: No restrictions

items.name: There's a New Boss in Town
restrictions: Must have completed "The Trickster's Delight (King of Shadows)"

The King of Shadows starts in the formation. He can't be moved or removed.
Only the King of Shadows and Champions in the two columns behind him can deal damage.
All boss enemies deal 100% additional damage.
A Mad Wizard has a 50% chance to spawn in the second wave of each non-boss area. No other enemies can be damaged while the Mad Wizard is alive.
Getting to Know The King of Shadows: The King of Shadows main support ability increases his damage and the damage of Champions in the two columns behind him.

items.name: Shadows of the Past
restrictions: Must have completed "The Trickster's Delight (King of Shadows)"

The King of Shadows starts in the formation. He can't be moved or removed.
Two Shadow escorts join the formation. 
Healing effects on Champions adjacent to one or more Shadows are reduced by 50%. 
Getting to Know The King of Shadows: The King of Shadows gains Tanking and DPS abilities that get stronger the longer it takes for him to be defeated. Use healers and tanking Champions with Taunt to have him last longer!

items.name: A Path Through the Astral Plane
restrictions: Must have completed "The Trickster's Delight (King of Shadows)"

The King of Shadows starts in the formation. He can't be moved or removed.
You may only use Elf, Dwarf, Half-Elf, Human, Evil, and/or Healing Champions.
1-2 Githyanki Warriors spawn with each wave. They deal 500% additional damage to the King of Shadows due to their silver swords, and they don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress.
Getting to Know The King of Shadows: The King of Shadows' second specialization choice determines which Champions he synergizes with best. Which set will you choose?

items.name: Free Play (King of Shadows)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Trickster's Delight (King of Shadows)"
No restrictions

items.name: Whispers of the Warlock
restrictions: Must have completed "The Crypt of Legends (Skylla)"

Skylla starts in the formation with her Whispers of Baba Yaga ability unlocked. She can be moved, but not removed. Only Skylla and Champions affected by her Whispers of Baba Yaga ability can deal damage. Getting to Know Skylla: Skylla increases the damage of Champions in the column in front of her, and her buff grows stronger the more Champions it affects. Put her behind the longest column buffing your best damage dealer!

items.name: Nocturli
restrictions: Must have completed "The Crypt of Legends (Skylla)"

Skylla starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. After area 50, enemies without a Debuff on them take only 1 damage from normal attacks and click damage. Two animated constructs join the formation. Champions next to these constructs attack 1 second faster. Getting to Know Skylla: When Skylla attacks and fails to defeat her enemy, she casts Faerie Fire on it, making it easier to damage with subsequent attacks. Use her and other debuff Champions to get through these resistant foes!

items.name: Twist of Fate
restrictions: Must have completed "The Crypt of Legends (Skylla)"

Skylla starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Debuff Champions, Evil Champions, or Champions with large differences in the ability scores that Skylla switches. Getting to Know Skylla: One of Skylla's specialization choices swaps the ability scores of Champions in the formation, and increases her buff when the ability scores differ by a large number. You might find some interesting interactions with Champions that care about ability score thresholds!

items.name: Free Play (Skylla)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Crypt of Legends (Skylla)"
No restrictions

items.name: No Autographs
restrictions: Must have completed "The Simril Spoilsport (Lark)" Getting to Know Lark: When Lark attacks an enemy and does not defeat it, he applies a debuff making it more susceptible to damage in the future. Use him and other debuff Champions to get through these resistant foes!

Lark starts in the formation with his Devil May Care ability unlocked. He can be moved, but not removed. Only Lark and Champions buffed by both him and Uggie can deal damage. 1-2 Unruly fans spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Lark and Uggie: Lark increases the damage of Champions in the middle columns, while Uggie increases the damage of Champions not next to her. Position your damage dealers to take advantage of both buffs!

items.name: Fortune's Fool
restrictions: Must have completed "The Simril Spoilsport (Lark)"

Lark starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Starting in area 51, enemies that don't have an active debuff reduce all normal attack damage to just 1 point of damage. Getting to Know Lark: When Lark attacks an enemy and does not defeat it, he applies a debuff making it more susceptible to damage in the future. Use him and other debuff Champions to get through these resistant foes!

items.name: All Together Now
restrictions: Must have completed "The Simril Spoilsport (Lark)"

Lark starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed.
You may only use Champions who are Fallbacks, Tieflings, Evil, multi-classed, or have a Charisma of 13 or lower.
Two backup musicians join the formation. They just get in the way.
Getting to Know Lark: Lark's specialization choice determines which sort of Champions he works best with. Build a formation that makes the most of his buffs!

items.name: Free Play (Lark)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Simril Spoilsport (Lark)"
No restrictions

items.name: Always Count Uggie
restrictions: Must have completed "The Merry Map Misadventure (Anson)"

Anson starts in the formation with his Unshakable Ability unlocked. He can be moved but not removed. Only Anson and Champions buffed by both him and Uggie can deal damage. 1-2 Mimics spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Anson: Anson increases the damage of Champions behind him, while Uggie increases the damage of Champions not next to Uggie. Position your damage dealer to take advantage of both buffs!

items.name: The Walkers of Waterdeep
restrictions: Must have completed "The Merry Map Misadventure (Anson)"

Anson starts in the formation. He can be moved but not removed. Champions don't recover health when moving to a new area. Champions resurrect at half health when changing areas instead of full health. Every 100 areas, enemy speed and damage is increased by 100%, stacking additively up to 10 times. Starting in area 50, a thrown rock hits a random Champion every second, dealing 1% of their max health in damage Getting to Know Anson: Anson's unstable weapon sometimes shocks him, but he powers through it. You may need to include some healing abilities to get him through!

items.name: Family Matters
restrictions: Must have completed "The Merry Map Misadventure (Anson)"

Anson starts in the formation. He can be moved but not removed. Anson's brother Valen and a scared villager join the formation. 1-2 Zhentarim Thugs spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. You may only use Good, Unaffiliated, or Fallbacks champions. Getting to Know Anson: Anson's specialization choices determine which heroes he works best with. Who will you include in the formation?

items.name: Free Play (Anson)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Merry Map Misadventure (Anson)"
No restrictions

items.name: The Crawling Dark
restrictions: Must have completed "The Missing Merchants (Kyre)"

Kyre starts in the formation. They can't be moved or removed. Only Kyre and Champions next to them can deal damage. 1-2 Shadowy Rats spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Kyre: Kyre increases the damage of Champions next to them. Place your damage dealer to take the best advantage of these buffs!

items.name: Absolutely Stunning
restrictions: Must have completed "The Missing Merchants (Kyre)"

Kyre starts in the formation. They can't be moved or removed. Champions deal 1% damage to enemies that are not stunned. A Monk joins the formation. Champions adjacent to the Monk have a 10% chance to stun enemies they attack for 5 seconds. 1-2 Shadow Goblins spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Kyre: Kyre can stun enemies and increase damage against them. Use them and other Champions that stun to get through these foes!

items.name: Critical Allies
restrictions: Must have completed "The Missing Merchants (Kyre)"

Kyre starts in the formation. They can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Good Champions, Control Champions, or Champions with a Dexterity of 16 or higher. After area 50, bosses spawn with a segmented special shield with 5 hits which must be destroyed before their regular hit points appear. To destroy a special shield segment, an enemy must be hit with a critical hit. 1-2 Shadows spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Kyre: Kyre's specialization choice determines which Champions they work best with. Who will you add to the formation?

items.name: Free Play (Kyre)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Missing Merchants (Kyre)"
No restrictions

items.name: The Sly One
restrictions: Must have completed "The Unfair Sea (Raistlin)"

Raistlin starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Only Raistlin and Champions not next to him can deal damage. After area 100, attacks against undamaged enemies have a 90% chance to completely miss. 1-2 Baaz Draconians spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Raistlin: Raistlin's role is dealing damage, and he deals more damage when he is next to fewer Champions. Place him in an isolated spot of a formation to increase his damage!

items.name: Test of Sorcery
restrictions: Must have completed "The Unfair Sea (Raistlin)"

Raistlin starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Fizban joins the formation. Champions in Fizban's column deal 1000% additional damage. This bonus counts as a positional formation ability. You may only use Champions that have positional formation abilities. In each boss area, a Sivak Draconian boss spawns as part of the first wave. It must also be defeated to progress. Getting to Know Raistlin: Raistlin's damage is increased dramatically when he is buffed by positional formation abilities that target 4 or fewer Champions in the formation. This buff further increases as the number of Champions buffed by the ability is reduced!

items.name: War of the Lance
restrictions: Must have completed "The Unfair Sea (Raistlin)"

Raistlin starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Good Champions, Tanking Champions, or Champions with a ranged or melee attack. Champions do not regain health when changing areas. Every 3 seconds, a random Champion is struck by a falling rock, dealing 5% of their maximum health in damage. 1-2 Kapak Draconians spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. In each boss area, an Aurak Draconian boss spawns as part of the first wave. It must also be defeated to progress. Getting to Know Raistlin: Raistlin's specialization choice determines which Champions he works best with. Which Champions will you add to the formation?

items.name: Free Play (Raistlin)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Unfair Sea (Raistlin)"
No restrictions

items.name: Did I Ever Tell You About...?
restrictions: Must have completed "A Fool's Errand (Tasslehoff)"

Tasslehoff starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Only Tasslehoff and Champions in the 3 rear-most columns can deal damage. Champions next to Tasslehoff attack slower due to his stories. Their base attack cooldowns are increased by 2 seconds. 1-2 Bozak Draconians spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Tasslehoff: Tasslehoff's buff can be set to one of the three columns in the back of the formation. Place your damage dealers to take advantage of this!

items.name: Fizban's Fabulous Friendship
restrictions: Must have completed "A Fool's Errand (Tasslehoff)"

Tasslehoff starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. Fizban joins the formation. Champions in Fizban's column deal 1000% additional damage. This bonus counts as a positional formation ability. You may only use Champions that have positional formation abilities. Only Tasslehoff and Champions not next to him can deal damage. 1-2 Baaz Draconians spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. In each boss area, a Sivak Draconian boss spawns as part of the first wave. It must also be defeated to progress. Getting to Know Tasslehoff: Tasslehoff's Diversion ability turns off positional formation buffs affecting his slot and neighboring slots, but his buff increases in power each time that happens. Place your damage dealer away from Tasslehoff to get the most of this!

items.name: A Loyal Friend
restrictions: Must have completed "A Fool's Errand (Tasslehoff)"

Tasslehoff starts in the formation. He can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Champions that count for any of Tasslehoff's second specialization choices. Only Tasslehoff and Champions not next to him can deal damage. The enemy/boss enrage bar starts out on the screen and begins to fill immediately. When one or more Monstrosity enemies are alive, the bar fills four times faster. 1-2 Kapak Draconians spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. In each boss area, an Aurak Draconian boss spawns as part of the first wave. It must also be defeated to progress. Getting to Know Tasslehoff: Tasslehoff's second specialization choice determines which Champions he works best with. Who will you choose?

items.name: Free Play (Tasslehoff)
restrictions: Must have completed "A Fool's Errand (Tasslehoff)"
No restrictions

items.name: Arrival of the Golden General
restrictions: Must have completed "The Mechanical Menace (Laurana)"

Laurana starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. After area 50, only Laurana and Champions targeted by her Battle Plan specialization choice can deal damage. 1-2 Baaz or Kapak Draconians spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. In each boss area, a Sivak Draconian boss spawns as part of the first wave. It must also be defeated to progress. Getting to Know Laurana: Laurana's first specialization choice selects one column behind her for her primary buff. Put your main damage dealer in that column!

items.name: Dragons of Winter Night
restrictions: Must have completed "The Mechanical Menace (Laurana)"

Laurana starts in the formation. She can't be moved or removed. Laurana gains 2 bonus Campaign stacks while on this adventure. Fizban joins the formation. Champions in Fizban's column deal 1000% additional damage. This bonus counts as a positional formation ability. 1-2 Chromatic Wyrmlings spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold, nor count towards quest progress. All enemies move 100% faster and deal 100% more damage. Getting to Know Laurana: Laurana has 15 bonus achievements, and her abilities improve as you complete these achievements. These achievements are listed in the Great Modron March section of the achievement dialog.

items.name: Critical Soldiers
restrictions: Must have completed "The Mechanical Menace (Laurana)"

Laurana starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. You may only use Heroes of the Lance Champions, DPS Champions, Hunter Champions, or Champions with a Constitution of 12 or lower. After area 50, bosses spawn with a special shield which must be destroyed before their regular hit points appear. To destroy a special shield, an enemy must be hit with a critical hit. After area 500, this special shield takes 5 critical hits to destroy. 1-2 Bozak Draconians spawn with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. In each boss area, an Aurak Draconian boss spawns as part of the first wave. It must also be defeated to progress. Getting to Know Laurana: Laurana's Soldiers further increase the power of her buff. Use her second specialization choice to add more Champions to her list of potential Soldiers!

items.name: Free Play (Laurana)
restrictions: Must have completed "The Mechanical Menace (Laurana)"
No restrictions

items.name: If You Can't Beat Them, Join Them
restrictions: Must have completed "Let Sleeping Dragons Lie (Trixie)"

Trixie the Pixie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. Only Trixie and Champions next to her can deal damage. 1-2 beasts attack with each wave. They don't drop gold nor count towards quest progress. Getting to Know Trixie the Pixie: Trixie increases the damage of Champions next to her. Place your main damage dealer next to her to get the most of her buff!

items.name: Size Matters Not
restrictions: Must have completed "Let Sleeping Dragons Lie (Trixie)"

Trixie the Pixie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. After area 50, you may only use Small Champions and all Champions that aren't Small are removed from the formation. Small Champions are fairy, gnome, goblin, halfling, kender, kobold, and plasmoid Champions. Non-boss enemies deal 100% additional damage and are increased in size by 50%. Getting to Know Trixie the Pixie: Trixie can reduce the size of two Champions in the slots directly behind her so they count as small. All Champions that share a species with one of those Champions are also reduced in size!

items.name: Little Force of Nature
restrictions: Must have completed "Let Sleeping Dragons Lie (Trixie)"

Trixie the Pixie starts in the formation. She can be moved, but not removed. After area 50, enemies that aren't slowed, stunned, or rooted only take 1 damage from normal attacks. Click damage only deals damage (including fire breath effects) for the first 25 areas. Fire breath potions can't be used during the adventure. Getting to Know Trixie the Pixie: Some variants turn off click damage, so familiars don't deal damage. You can still use familiars with Trixie's specialization choices to increase the attack rate of your other Champions!

items.name: Free Play (Trixie)
restrictions: Must have completed "Let Sleeping Dragons Lie (Trixie)"
No restrictions
